free_text: Energy and angular distributions of prompt neutrons
from slow neutron fission of U-235
Reference for energy
spectrum in lab. system , used in converting TOF
distributions to energy.
JEEP I reactor.
Slow neutron beam, collimated by a graphite and
steel collimator. 1.9 cm thick bismuth at inner end
of collimator to reduce gammas in beam. Collimated beam
with 13 mm at target position.
Slow neutron beam intensity at position of U-235 foil
was about 10**6 n/cm**2/sec.
Uranium target, enriched 94% in U-235, was vacuum
evaporated as UO2 on platinum backing, 24mm diameter,
1.24 mg/cm**2 thick.
Gas scintillation counter for fission fragments.
Gas was 10% xenon, 90% helium mixture.
Fragment collimator was made of duraluminum plate of
2.5mm thick; holes were 0.8mm diameter, distance
between holes 1.0 mm.
NE 102 plastic scintillator of 10.2 cm diameter,
4.0 cm thick as neutron detector.
Neutron flight path 90 cm from reactor concrete
block.
Neutron TOF distribution was converted to a pulse
amplitude spectrum. Time-to-amplitude converter was
calibrated by means of fission gamma-ray peak.
Typical coincidence counting rates were 16
counts/min at 0deg and 4 counts/min at 90 deg.
The background coincidence rate was obtained by putting
an iron shadow cone, 54 cm long, between target and
neutron counter.
The data of two series of measurements were treated
separately. Spectra corrected for background were
normalized to 2.E+07 fragment counts, or 10**7 light
(or heavy) fragment counts.
Correction for counting loss from one angle to another
was varying from 2.2% at O deg to 0.5% at 90 deg.
The counting loss was estimated to be 9% and was taken
into account in correction and normalization.
Correction for background.
An iterative least-squares program gave as a result:
35 % of neutrons are evaporated from heavy fragments,
50% - from light fragments and 15% are emitted  during
the fission process.
M.M.
SD: Heading ANG -> ANG-RL in Subent 5.
SD: REACTION code corrected in
Subents 002 - 004 following N.Otsuka's remark.
SD: REACTION code updated according to
last EXFOR rules in Subents 009,010.
SD: Subents 007 and 008 merged.

light fission fragment.
Errors are standard deviations as found from
counting statistics.
